footer.tagline: Explore Reality Through Science and Wisdom
footer.description: A careful, welcoming place for science, philosophy, contemplative practice, and open questions.
footer.closingLine: Curiosity with rigor. Practice with compassion.

newsletter.title: One thoughtful idea, every other Sunday.

metadata.title: About
metadata.description: The personal journey from a Vietnamese Buddhist childhood through technology, AI, and quantum curiosity that led to Qunara.

hero.lead: I began with questions that followed me from childhood, through technology, and into the unknown.
hero.artLabel: A quiet human silhouette stands before a deep night landscape and a restrained field of stars

childhood.quote: The sounds were familiar long before their meaning became clear.
childhood.p1: I grew up in a large Vietnamese family with a strong Buddhist tradition. My grandparents established a place of worship dedicated to Quan Âm in Vietnam. Quan Âm is the Vietnamese name for Avalokiteśvara, a Buddhist figure associated with compassion.
childhood.p2: Chanting and Buddhist ceremonies were familiar sounds in my childhood. They belonged to the rhythm of family life.
childhood.p3: But familiarity is not the same as understanding. At that age, I did not understand Buddhism deeply, and I did not pay much attention to it. It was part of my surroundings, not yet my personal journey.

technology.quote: Technology taught me how systems work. Life made me ask why we suffer within them.
technology.p1: As I grew older, I chose a different direction: information technology. For more than 25 years, I worked through different roles and wave after wave of change.
technology.p2: The field trained me to think in logic and systems. I learned to follow evidence, test an idea, study what failed, and try again.
technology.p3: Those habits helped me solve difficult technical problems. But personal and professional challenges raised other questions—questions that technology alone could not answer.

rediscovery.quote: The teachings had not changed. I had.
rediscovery.p1: Life brought success, uncertainty, and personal and professional storms. Over time, those experiences changed the way I heard teachings that had been present since childhood.
rediscovery.p2: Impermanence—the fact that everything changes—became visible in changes I could not prevent. The teaching on attachment helped me recognize the mind's habit of clinging to what cannot stay fixed.
rediscovery.p3: Suffering, perception—the way we interpret experience—and compassion became practical questions rather than distant religious ideas. I did not arrive at a sudden conversion. I simply began listening differently, little by little.

questions.quote: Science gave me new questions. Buddhism taught me to examine the mind asking them.
questions.p1: The questions about mind and experience later met another part of my life: artificial intelligence. As I became deeply involved in the AI revolution, I asked how information is represented, how computation processes it, and what we mean by consciousness—the capacity for experience.
questions.p2: That curiosity led me toward quantum physics. Quantum science challenged some everyday assumptions. Buddhist teachings questioned familiar ideas about permanence, separation, observation, and reality from a very different direction. I began to notice philosophical resonances—ideas that seemed to echo one another—as well as equally important differences.
questions.p3: Quantum physics does not prove Buddhism. Buddhist texts did not describe modern quantum mechanics. Placing them in conversation is an invitation to investigate carefully, not a scientific conclusion.

purpose.p1: I did not create Qunara because I had found all the answers. I created it because the questions became deeper, and because they deserved a thoughtful community.
purpose.p2: Here, people can learn quantum science, understand Buddhist philosophy, explore similarities, recognize important differences, examine evidence, practice awareness and compassion, and share thoughtful experiences and questions.
purpose.statement: Qunara is not an attempt to prove Buddhism through physics.
purpose.statementDetail: It is a place to explore what each tradition may teach us when approached with curiosity, evidence and humility.

principles.evidenceText: Scientific claims remain tied to scientific methods and sources.
principles.contextText: Buddhist teachings are presented within their historical and philosophical traditions.
principles.differenceText: Resemblance begins a conversation; it does not establish equivalence.
principles.reflectionText: Open questions are labeled rather than filled with certainty.
principles.practiceText: Awareness and compassion keep inquiry connected to human life.
principles.communityText: People may disagree while remaining careful, respectful, and willing to learn.

invitation.body: Qunara welcomes scientists, engineers, Buddhist practitioners, students, meditators, and curious people. Visitors do not need to agree. They need only to bring curiosity, respect, and a willingness to learn.
invitation.quote: The greatest discoveries are not only about the universe around us, but also about the universe within us.

art.childhoodLabel: A modest Vietnamese family shrine, a bell, incense, and palm leaves in warm evening light
art.technologyLabel: Calm architectural lines suggesting code, systems, and a human hand at a keyboard
art.returnLabel: A familiar path seen differently after a storm
art.quantumLabel: Wave interference and information patterns opening into a field of probabilities
art.communityLabel: Many quiet paths meeting beneath a lotus-like geometry of stars

metadata.title: Qunara — Explore Reality Through Science and Wisdom
metadata.description: A community exploring quantum physics, Buddhist philosophy, consciousness, and the practice of living wisely.

hero.lead: A community exploring quantum physics, Buddhist philosophy, consciousness, and the practice of living wisely.

science.title: Science
science.text: Clear, evidence-led guides to quantum mechanics, experiments, and the questions physics is still working to answer.

wisdom.title: Wisdom
wisdom.text: Buddhist philosophy in historical context—from impermanence and non-self to compassion and interdependence.

practice.title: Practice
practice.text: Meditation, reflection, and humane daily practices that turn interesting ideas into a wiser way of living.

feature.title: What the double-slit experiment actually tells us.
feature.description: The experiment is often used to make sweeping claims about consciousness. The real story—about interference, measurement, and probability—is more precise and more fascinating.

reflection.question: What did you notice today without immediately judging it?

discussion.title: Does observing always mean consciousness?
discussion.description: Community members untangle a common confusion around measurement.

meditation.title: Ten minutes with change
meditation.description: A quiet practice for noticing sensations arise, transform, and pass.
